Um, we wouldn't be able to commit to like a full year at this price at this point, the reason.
And I'll, I'll outline just what I have and I want the team members to, to join in and, and, and mention um and in and uh throw in any additional feedback that I might be missing.
But as far as I know, there's two things, one is that the concessions that are being applied are being applied.
Um There's the system's operating uh with the assumption that these leases are all 12 months.

So if a, if a building is offering a 234 month, well, let's say a two month or three month uh incentive, they're typically doing it on a longer lease uh commitment from the prospect, the uh but the system, when it's calculating that net uh effective rent, it's, it's just annually it to 12 months.
So it's e effectively over applying that uh that the, the dollar value of that incentive to the net rent and which, which artificially brings it down below what it's actually at.
So that's, that was item number one, which I'm happy to discuss further and, and you guys can let me know if this, if my information around that is, isn't, is not correct.

Um The second thing is that, you know, we also as part of our calls and what we're doing as far as our, like our manual process, which uses the spreadsheet, we're looking at not just the price but anything that is an incentive or any tools that these buildings are potentially using.
Um, in order to drive more traffic to the sites.
One of those tools is in New York, the, the landlords can increase the uh traffic by offering um what we call ops or broker commissions to outside brokers.

Um And that right now, as far as I know, is not something that's being included, which means that even if we did make the switch to apartment IQ today, um we would still be making those calls and that's really what I want.
I wanted if I really wanna get rid of those calls and get rid of the spreadsheet as much as everybody on the call.

But if we're still having to make those calls to make sure that we're getting um the whether or not the ops are being paid and how much um it's not ultimately saving us enough time for me to be willing to commit for a full year on a product.

Um Again, if we were looking at a shorter term commitment while we continue to work together on ironing out these things, that's, that's a totally different conversation in my opinion, but, um, given the kind of rigid kind of, um, uh, uh, parameters on, on the, the contract term of, of, of one year, um, it doesn't make sense for us to be able to, to get in on board with it yet.
Can I address the concerns right off the bat?
Because I, I have been working with your team.
Actually, Adam was really the one that hit home.
So I'm assuming he was the one that brought up the ops.
Um because we, we talked in depth about them.

And I'm not gonna pretend like it's not, we're, we're very much exploring it just like you're exploring us.
Uh So like if we decide to go all in there is an opportunity for us to start pulling those little p especially if they're listed in a centralized source.
Uh Right now, the reason that we don't do it is because nobody else in the entire country offers ops.
It's just not a thing.
Uh And this comes down to other New York centric features, like basically, like the more the more revenue we start to generate in New York, the more investment we put into New York.

So this would mean things like annualized square footages uh and ops and, and things that are, that are unique to the New York market that right now is really built for the national market with New York excluded.
So I don't want to say that those things are off the table.
But right now, like it, if like, it's one of those things where if we don't have people that move forward in New York, there's no reason to build that tool if that makes sense
totally.

And this is that I, I, if you remember at the very beginning of all this, this was my number one concern is that, listen, we get lots of Softwares that are, that are very successful across the country and then they come into New York and it's not because New York is special.
It's just they just, there's certain things that are just different right here.
And so you have to convince the people who are making these investments to invest in creating a customized system specifically for one market, right?
I get it.
I totally get it.
It's nobody's fault.
It's just that is,
this happens very often.

Um, so I just wanna reiterate that but also make sure that, you know, um I let you know that we, we really appreciate all the effort that you put into um to, to with each individual team.
Well,
sorry, go on Jamie,
you go ahead.

there.

Is there anywhere else that you're looking when you're pulling this information?

Ok.
Uh With the uh with the concessions, on the other hand, that product is being improved all the time.
In fact, if the concession has a listed lease term on it, it should be applying it to that lease term.

Now, the problem is, is when like if it says five or two months free rent on the front page, that's what's going to scrape and then if in the fine print on the unit, it's going to say one it's going to say, oh, on these four units, the system won't parse those two together because it's already captured on the front end and then we moved into the back end and then the concession is being applied.
Now, if it says can set like a free two month lease on 18 month leases, then the system will automatically apply that to 18 month leases.
So it, this is really what they're advertising to a renter.
So I want to be exceptionally clear here like when a renter is looking at this and they see two months free rent on that web page and they actually start to go in a funnel.
They are under the assumption that they're going to be able to get a 10 at 12 or 14, whatever it is without signing an 18 month lease to get that concession.
That's what's driving them into funnel.
You're pulling it off of street easy, right?
I mean, in a lot of cases, are you pulling any this the pricing data from Street Easy?
We pull
the pricing data from Street Easy, but the Concession Data Act, we still prioritize property websites because that's where the the most recent ones are going to go.
They, they usually feed on to Street Easy post that we check both sources is kind of what it comes down to prioritize properties.
Street

Easy advertises the gross rent, but typically in the same listing, they'll also list the net rent which is calculated typically using um an input, the two inputs, one is the value of that concession and the lease term upon which that concession is applied.

So I don't know if you could back solve using the net rent and the gross rent and do a little back solving to figure out what the actual lease term is or how, what all we need is the net effect of rent.
I mean, that's, that's all that really ultimately matters.
I mean, knowing the details of the concession is helpful for sure, but it we're basing pricing on our net effect on the net effect of rent that we're seeing out there.

Um You know, and if there are a lot of concessions and they're, they're heavy, but they're also on longer lease terms, that's, that is good information to know so that we, if we do decide to go down that road, we know how to structure it in a way that's competitive.
But so that maybe there is some publicly available information that you guys could use to.

that's what, that's what I was gonna say is like number one with the concessions solving against these term is on the road map, it just is like that is something that we're going to be doing nationally, not just in New York in New York, it might need a more bespoke solution since we like street easy only works in New York.
Uh But it could be as easy as just changing where the concession pulls from.
